title: Word problems with mass
description: Solve word problems involving mass.  Read the mass of items from an analog scale.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nikita borrows $1$ superhero book and $1$ dragon book from the library. 

The mass of the superhero book is shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

The dragon book has a mass of $310$ grams.

**What is the mass of the books Nikita borrowed combined?**
  
[[☃ input-number 3]] grams
hints: The mass of a superhero book is $\greenD{200}$ grams.
The mass of a dragon book is $\maroonD{310}$ grams.

We can find the total mass of the books by adding the mass of the superhero book $(\greenD{200\text{ g}})$ to the mass of the dragon book $(\maroonD{310\text{ g}})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{200}\text { grams} +  \maroonD{310}\text { grams} = \purpleD{510} \text { grams}$
The mass of the books is $510$ grams.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing a superhero book has 6 major tick marks. First major tick mark is labeled 0 and fourth major tick mark is labeled 150. Second, third, fifth, and sixth major tick marks are unlabeled. There are 4 minor unlabeled tick marks between every 2 major tick marks. The mass of the superhero book on the scale is at the fifth major unlabeled tick mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
Finn works at an aquarium. He weighs the shark and the sea lion (shown below).  

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

**What is the mass of the shark and the sea lion combined?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilograms
hints: The mass of the shark is $\greenD{650}$ kilograms.
The mass of the sea lion is $\maroonD{325}$ kilograms.

We can find the total mass of the animals by adding the mass of the shark $(\greenD{650\text{ kg}})$  to the mass of the sea lion $(\maroonD{325\text{ kg}})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{650}\text { kilograms} +  \maroonD{325}\text { kilograms} = \purpleD{975} \text { kilograms}$
The mass of the animals combined is $975$ kilograms.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing a shark with a starting major tick mark of 0 kilograms and the additional 7 major tick marks labeled 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, and 700. There are 3 minor unlabeled tick marks between every 2 major tick marks. The mass of the shark on the scale is at the second minor unlabeled tick mark after the major tick mark labeled 600.

A hanging scale weighing a sea lion with a starting major tick mark of 0 kilograms and the additional 7 major tick marks labeled 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, and 700. There are 3 minor unlabeled tick marks between every 2 major tick marks. The mass of the sea lion on the scale is at the first minor unlabeled tick mark after the major tick mark labeled 300.

assessment_items.question_content:
Olivia has a block of cheese (shown below). Sara came over and brought $84$ grams of cheese with her.

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many grams of cheese do Olivia and Sara have together?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints: Olivia's cheese has a mass of $\greenD{130}$ grams.
Sara brings a mass of $\maroonD{84}$ grams.

We can find the total mass of the cheese by adding the mass of the Olivia's cheese $(\greenD{130\text{ g}})$  to the mass of the Sara's cheese $(\maroonD{84\text{ g}})$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{130}\text { grams} +  \maroonD{84}\text { grams} = \purpleD{214} \text { grams}$
Olivia and Sara have $214$ grams of cheese.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing a block of cheese with a starting major tick mark of 0 grams and the additional 7 major tick marks labeled 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, 120, and 140. There is 1 minor unlabeled tick mark between every 2 major tick marks. The mass of the block of cheese on the scale is at the minor unlabeled tick mark after the major tick mark labeled 120.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nick is going on vacation. One of his bags weighs $24$ kilograms. The other bag weighs $17$ kilograms.

**How much do both of Nick's bags weigh combined?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 2]] kilograms
hints: The mass of the first bag is $\greenD{24}$ kilograms.
The mass of the second bag is $\maroonD{17}$ kilograms.

We can find the total mass of the bags by adding the mass of the first bag $( \greenD{24\text{ kg}})$  to the mass of the second bag $( \maroonD{17\text{ kg}})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{24}\text { kilograms} +  \maroonD{17}\text { kilograms} = \purpleD{41} \text { kilograms}$
The mass of the bags combined is $41$ kilograms.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jessica is making desserts. One box of cookies weighs $263$ grams. The brownies weigh $572$ grams.

**How much do both of the desserts weigh combined?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 2]] grams
hints: The mass of the box of cookies is $\greenD{263}$ grams.
The mass of the brownies is $\maroonD{572}$ grams.

We can find the total mass of the desserts by adding the mass of the box of cookies $( \greenD{263\text{ g}})$  to the mass of the brownies $( \maroonD{572\text{ g}})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{263}\text { grams} +  \maroonD{572}\text { grams} = \purpleD{835} \text { grams}$
The mass of the desserts combined is $835$ grams.

assessment_items.question_content:
Waldo's Water World found the mass of its two largest animals, a shark and a whale. 

The whale weighs $933$ kg, and the shark weighs $792$ kg.

**How much less is the mass of the shark than the whale?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 2]] kilograms
hints:
The mass of the shark is $\greenD{792}$ kilograms.  

The mass of the whale is $\purpleD{933}$ kilograms.

We can find how much less the mass of the shark is by subtracting the mass of the shark $(\greenD {792}\text{ kg})$ from the mass of the whale $(\purpleD{933}\text { kg})$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD{933}\text { kilograms}-\greenD{792}\text { kilograms} = \maroonD{141} \text { kilograms}$
The shark's mass is $141$ kilograms less than the whale's mass.

assessment_items.question_content:
A mouse and squirrel each found their mass (shown below) for the Forest Friends Wrestling Tournament.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

**How much less is the mass of the mouse than the squirrel?** 
 
[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints: The mass of the mouse is $\greenD{25}$ grams.
The mass of the squirrel is $\maroonD{255}$ grams.

We can find how much less the mass of the mouse is by subtracting the mass of mouse $(\greenD{25}\text{ g})$ from the mass of the squirrel $(\maroonD{255}\text { g})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\maroonD{255}\text { grams}-\greenD{25}\text { grams}= 230 \text { grams}$
The mouse's mass is $230$ grams less than the squirrel's mass.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing a mouse with a starting tick mark of 0 grams and the additional 5 tick marks labeled 50, 100, 150, 200, and 250. There are 9 unlabeled tick marks between every 2 labeled tick marks. The mass of the mouse on the scale is at the fifth unlabeled tick mark after tick mark labeled 0.

A hanging scale weighing a squirrel with a starting tick mark of 0 grams and the additional 5 tick marks labeled 50, 100, 150, 200, and 250. There are 9 unlabeled tick marks between every 2 labeled tick marks. The mass of the squirrel on the scale is at the first unlabeled tick mark after tick mark labeled 250.

assessment_items.question_content:
Every Monday a zoo finds the mass of its animals.   
 
This Monday, the zookeeper measured the monkey and the fox first (shown below).

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

**How much greater is the mass of the monkey than the fox?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints: The mass of the monkey is $\greenD{270}$ grams.
The mass of the fox is $\purpleD{230}$ grams.

We can find how much greater the mass of the monkey is by subtracting the mass of the fox $(\purpleD{230}\text{ g})$ from the mass of the monkey $(\greenD{270}\text { g})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{270}\text { grams}-\purpleD{230}\text { grams}= \maroonD{40}\text { grams}$
The monkey's mass is $40$ grams greater than the fox's mass.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing a monkey with a starting major tick mark of 0 grams and the additional 7 major tick marks labeled 50, 100, 150, 200, 250, 300, and 350. There are 4 minor unlabeled tick marks between every 2 major tick marks. The mass of the monkey on the scale is at the second minor unlabeled tick mark after the major tick mark labeled 250.

A hanging scale weighing a fox with a starting major tick mark of 0 grams and the additional 7 major tick marks labeled 50, 100, 150, 200, 250, 300, and 350. There are 4 minor unlabeled tick marks between every 2 major tick marks. The mass of the fox on the scale is at the third minor unlabeled tick mark after the major tick mark labeled 200.

assessment_items.question_content:
A walrus at the aquarium is weighed once a month. His weight for May is shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

In June, the walrus weighed $63$ kilograms less than he did in May.

**What did the walrus weigh in June? **  

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilograms
hints: The mass of the walrus in May is $\greenD{850}$ kilograms.
The walrus weighed $\purpleD{63}$ kilograms less in June than he did in May.

We can find the mass of the walrus in June by subtracting the weight the walrus lost $(\purpleD{63}\text { kg})$ from the mass of the walrus in May $(\greenD{850}\text{ kg})$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{850}\text { kilograms}-\purpleD{63}\text { kilograms}= \maroonD{787}\text { kilograms}$
The walrus weighed $787$ kilograms in June.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing a walrus with a starting major tick mark of 0 kilograms and the additional 9 major tick marks labeled 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, and 900. There is 1 minor unlabeled tick mark between every 2 major tick marks. The mass of the walrus on the scale is at the minor unlabeled tick mark after the major tick mark labeled 800.

assessment_items.question_content:
Irene finds the mass of her basketball is $587$ grams and her baseball is $145$ grams.

**How much greater is the mass of the basketball than the baseball?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints:
The mass of the basketball is $\green{587}$ grams.  

The mass of the baseball is $\purpleD{145}$ grams.

We can find how much greater the mass of the basketball is by subtracting the mass of the baseball $(\purpleD{145}\text{ g})$ from the mass of the basketball $(\greenD{587}\text { g})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{587}\text { grams}-\purpleD{145}\text { grams}= \maroonD{442} \text { grams}$
The basketball's mass is $442$ grams greater than the baseball's mass.

assessment_items.question_content:
Puppy palace has $9$ dog bones left at their store. All of the dog bones have the same mass.

The mass of $1$ bone is shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the total mass of $9$ dog bones?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilograms
hints: One dog bone has a mass of $\maroonD{2}$ kilograms.

We can find the mass of $\greenD9$ dog bones by multiplying $\greenD{9}$ by the mass of one bone $(\maroonD{2}\text{ kg})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{9}\text { bones} \times \maroonD{2}\text { kg per bone}$ $= \purpleD{18} \text { kg}$
$9$ dog bones have a mass of $18\text{ kg}$.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing a dog bone with a tick mark of 0 kilograms and the additional tick mark labeled 3. There are 2 unlabeled tick marks between 0 and 3 tick marks and there are 2 unlabeled tick marks after tick mark labeled 3. The mass of the dog bone on the scale is at the second unlabeled tick mark after the tick mark labeled 0.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $5$ penguins at the zoo. All of the penguins have the same mass.

The mass of $1$ penguin is shown below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the total mass of $5$  penguins?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilograms
hints: One penguin has a mass of $\maroonD{20}$ kilograms.

We can find the mass of $\greenD5$ penguins by multiplying $\greenD{5}$ by the mass of one penguin $(\maroonD{20}\text{ kg})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{5}\text { penguins} \times \maroonD{20}\text { kg per penguin} = \purpleD{100} \text { kg}$
$5$ penguins have a mass of $100\text{ kg}$.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing a penguin with a starting tick mark of 0 kilograms and an additional 9 tick marks. The fifth tick mark is labeled 25. The mass of the penguin on the scale is at the tick mark before the tick mark labeled 25.

assessment_items.question_content:
Emily needs $4$ eggs for baking her cake. All of the eggs have the same mass.

Emily knows the mass of $1$ egg (shown below).

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the total mass of $4$ eggs?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints: One egg has a mass of $\greenD{30}$ grams.

We can find the mass of $\blueD4$ eggs by multiplying $\blueD{4}$ by the mass of one egg $(\greenD{30}\text{ g})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\blueD{4}\text { eggs} \times \greenD{30}\text { grams per egg} = \maroonD{120} \text { grams}$
$4$ eggs have a mass of $120$ grams.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing an egg with a starting major tick mark of 0 grams and the additional 3 major tick marks labeled 25, 50, and 75. There are 4 minor unlabeled tick marks between every 2 major tick marks. The mass of the egg on the scale is at the first minor unlabeled tick mark after the major tick mark labeled 25.

assessment_items.question_content:
Budi builds a tower with $8$ blocks. All of his blocks have the same mass.

The mass of $1$ block is $10$ grams.

**What is the total mass of Budi's tower?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints: One block has a mass of $\greenD{10}$ grams.

We can find the mass of Budi's tower by multiplying $\blueD{8}$ blocks by the mass of one block $(\greenD{10}\text{ g})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\blueD{8}\text { blocks} \times \greenD{10}\text { grams per block}$ $= \maroonD{80} \text { grams}$
Budi's tower has a mass of $80$ grams.

assessment_items.question_content:
Tina has $5$ pencils. All of her pencils have the same mass.

Tina finds the mass of $1$ pencil is $8$ g.

**What is the total mass of $5$ pencils?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints: One pencil has a mass of $\greenD{8}$ grams.

We can find the mass of $\blueD5$ pencils by multiplying $\blueD{5}$ by the mass of one pencil $(\greenD{8}\text{ g})$.


[[☃ image 1]]
$\blueD{5}\text { pencils} \times \greenD{8}\text { grams per pencil}= \maroonD{40} \text { grams}$
$5$ pencils have a mass of $40$ grams.

assessment_items.question_content:
All of Glinda's magic wands have the same mass.

The mass of $1$ magic wand is $6$ grams.

**What is the total mass of $7$ magic wands?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints: One magic wand has a mass of $\maroonD{6}$ grams.

We can find the mass of $\blueD7$ wands by multiplying $\blueD{7}$ by the mass of one wand $(\maroonD{6}\text{ g})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\blueD{7}\text { wands} \times \maroonD{6}\text { grams per wand}$ $= 42 \text { grams}$
$7$ magic wands have a mass of $42$ grams.

assessment_items.question_content:
Smith's farm is raising pigs.

Each piglet has the same mass. One piglet's weight is $8$ kilograms. The weight of all the piglets is shown below. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**How many piglets are there?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] piglets
hints: The total mass of the piglets is $\greenD{40}$ kilograms.
One piglet's weight is $\maroonD8$ kilograms.

We can find the number of piglets by dividing the total number of kilograms $(\greenD{40}\text{ kg})$ by the weight of one piglet $(\maroonD8\text{ kg})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{40}\text { kg}\div\maroonD8\text { kg per piglet}= \blueD5 \text { piglets}$
There are $5$ piglets.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing piglets with a starting unlabeled tick mark and the additional 5 tick marks labeled 5, 15, 25, 35, and 45. There is one unlabeled tick mark between every 2 labeled tick marks. The mass of the piglets on the scale is at the unlabeled tick mark after the tick mark labeled 35.

assessment_items.question_content:
Computer World ships $10$ computer monitors at one time. The total mass of the shipment is $40$ kilograms.  

All of the monitors in the box have the same mass.

**What is the mass of one of the computer monitors?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] kilograms
hints:
The box has $\maroonD{10}$ monitors.

The total mass of $\maroonD{10}$ monitors is $\purpleD{40}$ kilograms.

We can find the mass of each monitor by dividing the total number of kilograms $(\purpleD{40}\text{ kg})$ by the number of monitors $(\greenD{10})$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\purpleD{40}\text{ kg} \div \greenD{10} \text{ monitors}= \maroonD{4}\text{ kg per monitor}$
One monitor has a mass of $4$ kilograms.

assessment_items.question_content:
All pizza slices at Pizza Party Zone have the same mass.

Tim buys a pizza there that has a total mass of $800$ grams. The pizza has $8$ slices.

**What is the mass of one of the pizza slices on Tim's pizza?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints: The pizza has $\maroonD8$ slices.
The total mass of $\maroonD8$ slices is $\greenD{800}$ grams.

We can find the mass of each pizza slice by dividing the total number of grams $(\greenD{800}\text{ g})$ by the number of pizza slices $(\maroonD8)$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{800}\text { grams} \div \maroonD8\text { slices} = \blueD{100} \text { grams per slice}$
One pizza slice has a mass of $100$ grams.

assessment_items.question_content:
All of the cheese slices at Chester's Cheesy Cafe have the same mass.

Chester finds that the total mass of $3$ cheese slices is $27$ grams.

**What is the mass of one of the cheese slices?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints: The total mass of the cheese slices is $\greenD{27}$ grams.
There are $\maroonD3$ slices.

We can find the mass of each cheese slice by dividing the total number of grams $(\greenD{27}\text{ g})$ by the number of cheese slices $(\maroonD3)$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{27}\text { grams}\div\maroonD3\text { cheese slices}= \blueD9 \text { grams per slice}$
One cheese slice has a mass of $9$ grams.

assessment_items.question_content:
All of Sophia Squirrel's acorns have the same mass.

Sophia Squirrel finds the total mass of her $4$ acorns (shown below).

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the mass of one of the acorns?**  

[[☃ input-number 1]] grams
hints: The total mass of $\maroonD4$ acorns is $\greenD{24}$ grams.

We can find the mass of each acorn by dividing the total number of grams $(\greenD{24}\text{ g})$ by the number of acorns $(\maroonD4)$.

[[☃ image 1]]
$\greenD{24}\text { grams}\div\maroonD4\text { acorns}= \blueD6\text { grams per acorn}$
One acorn has a mass of $6$ grams.

options.alt: A hanging scale weighing 4 acorns with a starting major tick mark of 0 grams and the additional 5 major tick marks labeled 5, 10, 15, 20, and 25. There are 4 minor unlabeled tick marks between every 2 major tick marks. The mass of the 4 acorns on the scale is at the fourth minor unlabeled tick mark after the major tick mark labeled 20.
